title: Carrier Global vs. Owens Corning: Which Industrials Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: Carrier Global and Owens Corning are compared as infrastructure investment options. Carrier Global is pivoting toward climate solutions with strong AI data center demand (500%+ growth), while Owens Corning faces headwinds from construction cyclicality and a recent net loss. Carrier trades at a forward P/E of 24x (below sector average of 30.4x), while Owens Corning is cheaper at 12.5x but struggles with weaker residential demand and customer concentration risks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong growth in AI data center business (500%+ increase), expanding heat pump demand in Europe, strategic pivot to higher-margin climate solutions, and valuation below sector average despite revenue decline. Management expects AI sector to contribute over $1.5 billion in revenue.

Reported net loss of $522 million in FY 2025 with negative 5.2% net margin, revenue declined 8%, high customer concentration risk (two customers represent 28% of sales), exposure to construction cyclicality, and litigation risks from fire safety issues with Paroc subsidiary.
Mentioned as a major competitor to Carrier Global in the HVAC and climate solutions market, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.
Mentioned as a competitor to Carrier Global, but no specific performance analysis provided. The Motley Fool has positions in this company.
Mentioned as a competitor to Owens Corning in building materials, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.

title: HVAC Market Opportunity Report 2026: Surge in Urban Development, Energy Efficiency, and Smart Technologies Drive Expansion - Growth Analysis and Forecasts 2025-2035
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global HVAC market is projected to grow from USD 524.9 billion in 2025 to USD 1.2 trillion by 2035, driven by rapid urbanization, energy efficiency demands, and smart technology adoption. The air conditioning equipment segment leads with USD 215.8 billion in 2025, while the indirect distribution channel dominates with 74% market share. The U.S. market alone is valued at USD 156.9 billion and expected to grow at 10.2% CAGR.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major HVAC manufacturer benefiting from increased demand for energy-efficient cooling solutions and smart HVAC technologies in expanding markets.
Major HVAC manufacturer well-positioned in a market expected to reach USD 1.2 trillion by 2035 with strong growth in energy-efficient systems.
Major HVAC manufacturer positioned to benefit from strong market growth at 8.1% CAGR and increasing demand for advanced environmental control systems.

title: LiquidStack GigaModular™ CDU Platform Is Now Commercially Available with Expanded 14 MW Capacity
author: Na
description: LiquidStack, a Trane Technologies company, announced the commercial availability of its GigaModular™ CDU platform, a modular liquid cooling solution designed for high-density AI infrastructure. The platform scales up to 14 MW with pay-as-you-grow architecture, centralized controls, and support for next-generation GPU platforms. The system has completed multi-module testing and achieved ETL certification, with early customer orders already received.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of LiquidStack, Trane benefits from the successful product launch and expansion into the high-growth AI infrastructure market. The announcement demonstrates the company's strategic positioning in thermal management solutions for emerging technology sectors.

title: Vertiv: The Backlog is Massive, the Margin of Safety is Not
author: Gurufocus
description: Vertiv (VRT), a leader in data center power and thermal infrastructure for AI, has surged 300% in 12 months with a massive $15 billion backlog and improving unit economics. However, the stock trades at a 50.9x forward P/E multiple with only a 1.9% FCF yield, leaving minimal margin of safety despite strong fundamentals and growth prospects. The valuation assumes sustained high growth and successful services transformation, with execution risks from project delays and financing constraints.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong business fundamentals with 28% YoY revenue growth, 20.4% operating margins, and structural positioning in AI infrastructure. However, valuation at 50.9x forward P/E is stretched with minimal margin of safety. The article acknowledges exceptional business quality but advises against adding at current levels due to limited downside protection.
Mentioned as a major hyperscaler with $650B+ collective capex guidance for 2026 alongside Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, supporting strong demand for Vertiv's infrastructure products.
Mentioned as a major hyperscaler with $650B+ collective capex guidance for 2026 alongside Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft, supporting strong demand for Vertiv's infrastructure products.
Major hyperscaler with significant capex commitments supporting AI infrastructure buildout and demand for Vertiv's solutions.
Key hyperscaler with substantial capex guidance supporting the AI infrastructure cycle that benefits Vertiv.
Major hyperscaler with significant capex commitments driving demand for data center power and cooling infrastructure.
Vertiv has deep ties with NVIDIA, providing early visibility into hardware roadmaps and engineering solutions in parallel with new GPU platforms, creating structural competitive advantage.
Direct competitor in power management and thermal infrastructure benefiting from data center buildout, but growing 4.4x slower than Vertiv with lower valuation multiples (28x forward P/E).
Competitor in power management benefiting from same data center cycle but growing 2.6x slower than Vertiv with lower valuation (30.5x forward P/E).
Peer company in thermal infrastructure with lower valuation multiple (32.1x forward P/E) and slower growth compared to Vertiv.

title: You Just Missed The SanDisk And Micron Memory Trade: These 10 Stocks Could Ride The Next AI Wave
author: Piero Cingari
description: As memory stocks like Micron and SanDisk have already surged dramatically, investors are shifting focus to liquid cooling infrastructure for AI data centers. With AI factories scaling to gigawatt levels and thermal management accounting for $55-82.5 billion of the U.S. market through 2030, companies providing cooling solutions, heat exchangers, and related thermal equipment are positioned as the next major AI beneficiaries.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO reported meaningful 2027 orders for data center cooling with capacity available; global data center orders rose 500% YoY; backlog covers full 2026 sales target of $1.5B
Record Q1 bookings of $6.7B (+27%), backlog at $10.7B (+30% YoY); customer lead times stretched to 12-18 months; acquired Liquidstack for two-phase cooling exposure
Acquired CoolIT Systems for $4.75B; CoolIT growing near triple-digit YoY with expected $550M sales addition; leading vendor in direct-to-chip liquid cooling
$9.5B Boyd Thermal acquisition provides full-stack liquid cooling exposure for AI data centers
Pure-play data center thermal pivot with chillers and liquid cooling solutions positioned for significant market growth
Accelcius investment in January provides two-phase liquid cooling exposure for AI infrastructure
SWEP brazed heat plates hold majority share inside cooling distribution units, critical component in thermal management stack
Two-phase fluid supplier into the cooling stack; positioned to benefit from massive thermal management market expansion
April IPO providing customized hyperscaler thermal systems; early-stage player in growing liquid cooling market
Memory trade already captured with 700% gains in 12 months; article suggests investors have missed this wave and should focus on cooling infrastructure instead
Memory trade already captured with 3,600% gains; article positions this as a completed trade cycle, not a forward opportunity
Next-generation Vera Rubin racks (155-200 kW) and speculative Kyber racks (500+ kW) require liquid cooling as standard; driving demand for thermal solutions
